Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1. Discover the Airtable base and table structure using airtable_list_bases (no parameters) to find base appMoveOpsOps001, then airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) to identify the Relocations table (tblRelocations01) where relocation records are stored.
OE 2. Search Airtable for the three crisis relocations using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Name) with value Kowalski, then Mitchell, then Whitfield. Each search returns two records per employee: a pre-crisis record (recReloc00000000037 for Kowalski, recReloc00000000038 for Mitchell, recReloc00000000035 for Whitfield) still showing the original In Progress status and no vendor crisis notes, and a crisis-updated record (recReloc_kowalski_crisis, recReloc_mitchell_crisis, recReloc_whitfield_crisis) showing the emergency vendors but still stuck at In Progress despite deliveries being complete. The crisis records show Kowalski was split between KeyMove and National Van Lines, Mitchell was handled by National Van Lines, and Whitfield was handled by National Van Lines with a 1-day delay.
OE 3. Search for Craig Nguyen's email using search_emails (query: Craig Nguyen) or search_emails (query: KeyMove). Finds email_craig_chloe_pickup_01 from craig.nguyen@keymovespecialty.com with subject "Kowalski Specialty Pickup Complete — All Items Secured" confirming all robotics equipment was secured and delivered, and offering a standing arrangement for specialty transport going forward. This is the triggering email the prompt references.
OE 4. Search Slack for coordinator handoff documentation using conversations_search_messages (search_query: Fatimah), then (search_query: Suki), then (search_query: Javier), and optionally (search_query: Swift crisis) or (search_query: Kowalski). Finds the crisis thread in channel C006 where Fatimah flagged Swift's website going down and a driver reporting unpaid wages, Javier confirmed National Van Lines booking for Whitfield at 3200 dollars, and Suki researched backup vendor options. Also finds Chloe's crisis plan message assigning each coordinator their role. No coordinator flagged unresolved post-delivery issues.
OE 5. Search for post-delivery client follow-ups using search_emails (query: Tamara Byrd) and search_emails (query: Tessa Moreno). Finds Tamara Byrd's pre-delivery concern email (email_tamara_chloe_concern_01) about Kowalski and Mitchell equipment safety, and Chloe's reply reassuring her (email_chloe_tamara_reassure_01). Finds Tessa Moreno's pre-delivery email (email_tessa_chloe_delay_01) asking about Jordan Whitfield's pickup delay and whether Preethi Nair's upcoming Atlanta to Raleigh move would be affected, and Chloe's reply (email_chloe_tessa_reassure_01). No post-delivery complaint emails found from either client. The agent should recognize that silence after delivery is not the same as confirmation of satisfaction.
OE 6. Search Linear for the Swift crisis and DOT compliance tickets using linear_list_issues (query: Swift crisis) or (query: vendor replacement) or (query: DOT Tanaka). Finds three key issues: the parent crisis ticket issue_swift_vendor_crisis_01 (number 340, priority Urgent, state in_progress, assigned to Chloe), the vendor replacement sub-issue issue_vendor_replacement_search (number 341, due 2026-05-02, listing National Van Lines, Allied Moving Services, and Meridian Global Mobility as candidates), and the DOT cert gap issue_dot_cert_swift_dependency (number 342, due 2026-04-28, noting that Swift was the only source for Keiko Tanaka's Class 3B laser placarding certificate and no alternative has been identified). The agent discovers that the DOT cert due date is April 28, which is imminent given the current post-April-24 timeframe, and that none of the three listed alternative options have been pursued yet.
OE 7. Optionally retrieve comments on the crisis ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: issue_swift_vendor_crisis_01). Finds three status updates confirming all pickups completed April 22, all deliveries completed by April 24, and total emergency cost of 10050 dollars. The financial breakdown (approximately 4800 dollars in original Swift quotes, approximately 5250 dollars net overage) is available from Slack messages in the operations channel (C006) and Chloe's financial summary email to Marcus (email_chloe_marcus_finance_01), not from the Linear comments themselves.
OE 8. Update the three crisis Airtable records to Completed status using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records array containing the three crisis record IDs). Each record's Status field must be set to Completed (not Delivered, which is not a valid singleSelect choice; the valid options are Inquiry, In Progress, Completed, and Cancelled). The Special Requirements field should be updated to reflect the crisis resolution with actual vendors used and delivery confirmation. The agent must also handle the three pre-crisis duplicate records, either by deleting them using airtable_delete_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, record_ids: recReloc00000000037, recReloc00000000038, recReloc00000000035) or by updating their status to Cancelled with a superseded note.
OE 9. Reply to Craig Nguyen's email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_craig_chloe_pickup_01, sender: chloe.vance@moveops.com, content: acknowledging the Kowalski delivery, expressing interest in the standing arrangement, requesting a formal proposal by May 2 covering service capabilities and rate structure, and specifically asking whether KeyMove holds DOT or PHMSA hazmat certification for placarding). The hazmat question is an implicit requirement because Chloe knows the Keiko Tanaka cert gap needs an alternative source and KeyMove is a potential path.
OE 10. Update the vendor replacement Linear ticket using linear_update_issue (id: issue_vendor_replacement_search) to add KeyMove Specialty Transport as a candidate alongside the existing entries for National Van Lines, Allied Moving Services, and Meridian Global Mobility. The description should note KeyMove's proven performance on the Kowalski emergency, Craig's standing arrangement offer, the proposal deadline of May 2, and the open question about hazmat certification capabilities.
OE 11. Close the crisis tracking ticket using linear_update_issue (id: issue_swift_vendor_crisis_01, state: Done). Optionally add a closing comment via linear_create_comment (issueId: issue_swift_vendor_crisis_01, body: summary of all three deliveries confirmed, Airtable records cleaned up, client status, and pointers to remaining open sub-issues 341, 342, and 343).
OE 12. Update the DOT compliance ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: issue_dot_cert_swift_dependency, body: status update noting that the placarding cert was never received before Swift went dark, hazmat training scheduled for April 24, no alternative source identified yet, and KeyMove being explored as a potential path via the vendor proposal). This documents the current gap and keeps the ticket current.
OE 13. Look up the operations Slack channel using channels_list (channel_types: public_channel) to find channel C006 for operations.
OE 14. Post a team briefing in the operations channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006, user_id: moveops_chloe_vance, payload: comprehensive update covering all three delivery confirmations with actual vendors and dates, Airtable cleanup status, client follow-up gaps for Tamara Byrd and Tessa Moreno, coordinator action items for Fatimah and Suki and Javier to send proactive post-delivery check-ins, the DOT compliance cert gap and its urgency, vendor replacement candidates with the May 2 proposal deadline, and interim vendor routing guidance for coordinators heading into May).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 11. Close the crisis tracking ticket using linear_update_issue (id: issue_swift_vendor_crisis_01, state: Done). OE 12. 1 Airtable relocation records for Lena Kowalski, Andre Mitchell, and Jordan Whitfield were updated (via airtable_update_records) with the Status field set to Completed.
outcome

✓Run #1The agent called airtable_update_records on table tblRelocations01 in base appMoveOpsOps001 with records recReloc_kowalski_crisis, recReloc_mitchell_crisis, and recReloc_whitfield_crisis, each with Status set to 'Completed'.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_update_records on table tblRelocations01 with Status set to 'Completed' for all three records: recReloc_whitfield_crisis, recReloc_kowalski_crisis, and recReloc_mitchell_crisis.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records with records including recReloc_kowalski_crisis, recReloc_mitchell_crisis, and recReloc_whitfield_crisis, all with Status set to 'Completed'. The tool result confirms the updates were applied successfully.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call targeting tblRelocations01 in base appMoveOpsOps001 with records recReloc_kowalski_crisis, recReloc_mitchell_crisis, and recReloc_whitfield_crisis all set to Status: 'Completed'. This is confirmed in the tool result output.
✗Run #5The agent set Status to 'Delivered' in the airtable_update_records call, not 'Completed'. The confirmed result in tool_call_result_18.json shows Status: 'Delivered' for all three records. 'Delivered' is not a valid singleSelect option per the criterion; the correct value is 'Completed'.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_update_records on table tblRelocations01 in base appMoveOpsOps001 with records recReloc_kowalski_crisis, recReloc_mitchell_crisis, and recReloc_whitfield_crisis all having Status set to 'Completed'.
2 The Airtable update for the three crisis relocation records includes updated Special Requirements text that references the actual emergency vendors used (KeyMove Specialty Transport for Kowalski and National Van Lines for all three moves, or similar)
outcome

✓Run #1The airtable_update_records call included updated Special Requirements fields for all three records. Kowalski's notes mention 'KeyMove Specialty Transport' for equipment and 'National Van Lines' for household goods. Mitchell and Whitfield both reference 'National Van Lines' as the emergency vendor, replacing the original Swift references.
✓Run #2The Special Requirements fields were updated with vendor crisis resolution narrative. Kowalski mentions KeyMove Specialty Transport for robotics and National Van Lines for household. Mitchell and Whitfield both mention National Van Lines. Swift references are replaced with the actual emergency vendors used.
✓Run #3The airtable_update_records call included detailed Special Requirements text for all three records referencing the actual emergency vendors: KeyMove Specialty Transport (Craig Nguyen) for Kowalski's robotics equipment and National Van Lines for household goods, and National Van Lines for both Mitchell and Whitfield.
✓Run #4The airtable_update_records call updates Special Requirements for all three crisis records with detailed crisis resolution notes referencing the actual emergency vendors: KeyMove Specialty Transport for Kowalski's equipment, National Van Lines for all household goods/full moves, and specific rates and delivery dates.
✓Run #5The airtable_update_records call updated the Special Requirements field for all three crisis records. Kowalski's record references KeyMove Specialty Transport and National Van Lines. Mitchell and Whitfield reference National Van Lines. All mention the Swift crisis resolution context, replacing any pre-crisis vendor notes.
✓Run #6The airtable_update_records call for all three crisis records included updated Special Requirements fields explicitly mentioning the emergency vendors: KeyMove Specialty Transport for Kowalski's robotics equipment and National Van Lines for household goods/all moves. Crisis resolution context was provided in each record.
3 The three pre-crisis duplicate Airtable relocation records (recReloc00000000037 for Kowalski, recReloc00000000038 for Mitchell, recReloc00000000035 for Whitfield) were either deleted (via airtable_delete_records) or updated to Cancelled status (via airtable_update_records)
outcome

✗Run #4The agent updated the pre-crisis records (recReloc00000000037, recReloc00000000038, recReloc00000000035) with Status set to 'Completed' (with 'SUPERSEDED' notes), not 'Cancelled'. The criterion requires either deletion or Cancelled status. Using Completed for superseded/duplicate records does not satisfy the requirement.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_delete_records with record_ids ['recReloc00000000037', 'recReloc00000000038', 'recReloc00000000035'], which matches exactly the three pre-crisis duplicate records. The tool result confirmed all three were deleted.
